nodes.label: Tests for agent/curator.py — orchestrator, idle gating, state transitions.  LLM
Isolated MANGABA_HOME + freshly reloaded curator + skill_usage modules.
The FIRST observation of the curator (fresh install, no state file)     must NOT
A run older than the configured interval should re-trigger. Use a     2x-interva
A freshly-created skill with no use history should not get archived     just bec
Manual skills can have usage records, but without the agent-created     marker t
Dry-run previews must not bump last_run_at or run_count. A preview     shouldn't
The dry-run prompt must carry a banner instructing the LLM not to     call any m
Dry-run must not call apply_automatic_transitions — the auto pass     archives s
Fresh install (no curator state file) must NOT fire the curator on     the first
Core invariants must be in the review prompt text.
The review prompt must rely on existing tools (skill_manage + terminal)     and
Pinning is a user opt-out, not a model decision. The prompt should     not tell
The curator prompt must push umbrella-building / class-level thinking,     not p
Support-file demotion (references/templates/scripts) must be one of     the thre
mangaba curator unpin must refuse bundled/hub skills too (matches pin).
auxiliary.curator absent (or auto/empty) → use main model.provider/model.
auxiliary.curator.{provider,model} fully set → that pair wins.
Per-slot api_key/base_url must ride into resolve_runtime_provider (not main-only
Falling through to main model must not pick up stray auxiliary.curator secrets.
Only one of slot provider/model set → fall back to the main pair.      Prevents
Pre-unification users set curator.auxiliary.{provider,model} — honor it.      Em
When BOTH new and legacy are set, the canonical slot wins.
Missing auxiliary/curator sections never raise — fall back cleanly.
Curator must be a first-class slot in every aux-task registry.      Four sources
